articles.title: Analysis | Taking a gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone's analyzing whether prediction markets are worth the risk, probably weighing their accuracy against traditional polls or expert forecasts. The timing matters because mainstream media coverage like this drives new money into prediction markets, which means more liquidity and potentially better odds for sharp bettors.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Predicting This Year’s NBA Finals Champion According to Kalshi Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Kalshi's prediction markets are pricing the odds for which NBA team will win the 2024-25 championship, giving us a real-money take on title favorites beyond traditional sportsbooks. These markets offer a cleaner signal than betting odds since they're not skewed by public action chasing popular teams, making them valuable for spotting value bets or gauging true championship probabilities.

articles.title: The same Polymarket trader who predicted the start of the Iran war is now betting on a cease-fire by next week
articles.summary: A Polymarket whale who correctly called the timing of Iran's recent escalation is now placing heavy bets on a cease-fire materializing within the next seven days. When traders with proven track records on geopolitical events start taking contrarian positions, it's either a signal worth following or a spectacular way to lose your shirt.

articles.title: Kalshi Hits Record $3.4B Week, Polymarket Sports Surge as March Madness Heats Up
articles.summary: Kalshi smashed records with $3.4B in weekly volume while Polymarket's sports betting exploded during March Madness tournament action. The surge shows prediction markets are finally capturing mainstream betting dollars during major sporting events, potentially marking their transition from niche political curiosity to serious sportsbook competitor.

articles.title: Polymarket Curbs Insider Bets: No Stolen Info, No Illegal Tips, No Outcome Insiders
articles.summary: Polymarket just banned users from betting on events where they have inside information, stolen data, or advance knowledge of outcomes. The crackdown signals the platform is trying to legitimize prediction markets by preventing the kind of information asymmetries that make regular bettors into suckers.

articles.title: Experts project an OKC blow out, but will Kalshi markets buy shorthanded Sixers keep it closer?
articles.summary: The Thunder are heavy favorites against a depleted 76ers squad tonight, but Kalshi traders might be finding value in Philadelphia keeping the game competitive despite missing key players. When expert consensus diverges this sharply from market sentiment, it creates exactly the kind of mispricing opportunity that sharp bettors live for.

articles.title: Exclusive | Lawmakers to Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Bipartisan lawmakers are preparing legislation that would prohibit prediction markets from offering bets on sporting events. The bill threatens to cut off a major revenue stream for platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, potentially forcing them to rely entirely on political and economic events for liquidity.

articles.title: Inside the world of political betting: ‘My job for the rest of my life’
articles.summary: A bettor has turned political wagering into their full-time career, suggesting the market has matured enough to support professional gamblers who treat elections like sports. This signals political betting is evolving from a hobby into a legitimate income source, which means more sophisticated analysis and potentially tighter spreads as pros crowd out casual money.

articles.title: Opinion | The Casino That’s Eating the World
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This matters because as markets gain mainstream adoption, pushback from traditional institutions and policymakers could shape how much influence they're allowed to have in everything from corporate strategy to government policy.

articles.title: If one trader can force the outcome of a prediction market, it shouldn’t be tradable
articles.summary: A single trader with enough capital can manipulate prediction markets by forcing outcomes through their actions rather than predicting them, essentially breaking the core mechanism that makes these markets valuable. This exposes a fundamental flaw in how we design prediction markets, since allowing outcome manipulation turns what should be information aggregation into expensive gambling on who has the deepest pockets.

articles.title: Prediction markets' latest 'Wild West' accusations: Users are getting suckered by insider trading
articles.summary: Critics are claiming prediction markets have become a free-for-all where insiders with privileged information are cleaning out regular users who don't know they're playing a rigged game. If these accusations stick, they could trigger the kind of regulatory crackdown that would either force platforms to implement costly safeguards or scare away the retail money that keeps these markets liquid.
